content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 8, 1993


Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
The International Building
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

RE: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-93-140

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because your questions concern past
conduct, we treat your request as one for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Your letter dated March 30, 1993, correctly summarizes my
advice regarding Mr. Morally's filing obligation as a city
independent expenditure committee for the first half of 1992 and a
county independent expenditure committee for the second half of
the year. Because Mr. Morally made independent expenditures
totaling $1,000 or more during the first half of 1992 to support
the qualification of a measure in the City of San Marcos, he was
required to file an Independent Expenditure Committee and Major
Donor Committee Campaign Statement (Form 461) with the San Marcos
City Clerk. (Sections 82013, 84200.)

According to your letter, Mr. Morally then made a
contribution in the City of San Diego during the second half of
1992, which by definition made him a "county" committee required


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Peter Bagatelos
I-93-140
Page 2

to file Form 461 with the County of San Diego. In addition, Mr.
Morally was required to continue his filing obligation with the
City of San Marcos for the second half of the year. (Sections
82027.5, 84215.)3/

However, your letter does not accurately describe our
discussions regarding Mr. Morally's filing obligation regarding
the Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report (Form 465).

It is my understanding that Mr. Morally made independent
expenditures totaling $1,000 or more on behalf of a San Marcos
city initiative measure that did not qualify for the ballot.
Therefore, Mr. Morally qualified as an independent expenditure
committee pursuant to Section 82013(b). All independent
expenditure committees are treated as general purpose committees
under the Act and do not qualify as primarily formed committees.
(Section 82027.5(a).)

The requirement to file the Supplemental Independent
Expenditure Report is contained in Section 84203.5(a), which
provides:

In addition to any campaign statements required
by this article, if a candidate or committee has made
independent expenditures totaling five hundred dollars
($500) or more in a calendar year to support or oppose a
candidate, a measure or qualification of a measure, it
shall file independent expenditure reports at the same
time, covering the same periods, and in the places where
the candidate or committee would be required to file
campaign statements under this article, as if it were
formed or existing primarily to support or oppose the
candidate or measure or qualification of the measure. No
independent expenditure report need be filed to cover a
period for which there has been no activity to report.

(Emphasis added.)

Therefore, because Mr. Morally spent $1,000 or more to oppose
the initiative, both Form 461 and Form 465 were required to be
filed in the City of San Marcos.

3/ It should also be noted, however, the Commission has advised
that under certain circumstances, an occasional contribution to a
candidate or measure in another jurisdiction will not alter a
committee's filing status. (Whitaker Advice Letter No. A-87-115.)

Peter Bagatelos
I-93-140
Page 3

     If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Linda Moureaux
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for an individual who made independent expenditures in multiple jurisdictions during different periods in 1992, specifically regarding Form 461 (Independent Expenditure Committee and Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement) and Form 465 (Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report) requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mr. Morally was required to file Form 461 with the City of San Marcos for the first half of 1992 and with the County of San Diego for the second half of 1992, while continuing his filing obligation with San Marcos for the second half. Additionally, both Form 461 and Form 465 were required to be filed in San Marcos because he spent $1,000 or more on independent expenditures for a local initiative measure.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document is a confirmation of prior telephone advice treated as informal assistance per Regulation 18329(c). Requested Q/A format not present; instead advisor provides confirmation and corrections to requestor's summary. Advice concerns campaign disclosure requirements under Political Reform Act (Gov. Code §81000-91015). Form references: 461 (Independent Expenditure Committee and Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement) and 465 (Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report). Key statute cited: Section 84203.5(a).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for an individual who made independent expenditures in multiple jurisdictions during different periods in 1992, specifically regarding Form 461 (Independent Expenditure Committee and Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement) and Form 465 (Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report) requirements?

CONCLUSION: Mr. Morally was required to file Form 461 with the City of San Marcos for the first half of 1992 and with the County of San Diego for the second half of 1992, while continuing his filing obligation with San Marcos for the second half. Additionally, both Form 461 and Form 465 were required to be filed in San Marcos because he spent $1,000 or more on independent expenditures for a local initiative measure.

embedding.summary: This informal advice letter confirms telephone guidance provided to Mr. Bagatelos regarding Mr. Morally's campaign disclosure filing obligations as an independent expenditure committee under the California Political Reform Act, clarifying which forms and jurisdictions required filing based on his expenditure activities in 1992.
